ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: High quality, text-based PDF. All fields clearly legible.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: High quality, text-based PDF. All fields clearly legible. CAMAB logo/branding at top.

lc_check.lc_context_analysis: The certificate contains 'LC NO: LC0999323000011' in box 6 (goods description). The invoice contains 'L/C NO LC0999323000011'. However, per rule 2.2 CRITICAL RULE – Banktermskontext, I must verify whether this constitutes a genuine Letter of Credit reference or merely a reference number containing the letters 'LC'. The certificate field reads 'LC NO:' which is a SEPARATE field label/etikett followed by a number. The invoice reads 'L/C NO' which is an explicit bank-term designation. Both use recognized LC-terminology ('LC NO', 'L/C NO') as separate labels, not as part of a reference number. The LC numbers match exactly: LC0999323000011. This satisfies the requirements for LC-undantag activation.

lc_validation.conclusion: LC-undantag activated. The text 'LC NO:' is a separate field label followed by the LC number LC0999323000011. This matches the invoice's 'L/C NO LC0999323000011'. Verification limited to country of origin per rule 2.2.

country_of_origin.motivation: Certifikatet anger ursprungsland 'NORWAY' i box 3. Fakturan anger 'NORWAY' i det dedikerade ORIGIN-fältet. Dessa överensstämmer exakt utan normalisering. Ursprungslandet är verifierat.

comparison_steps.action: Verify origin field is explicit and dedicated
comparison_steps.result: The invoice has a dedicated 'ORIGIN' field header with value 'NORWAY'. This satisfies the LC requirement for explicit origin field.

country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3 states 'NORWAY'. Invoice has a dedicated ORIGIN field stating 'NORWAY'. Exact textual match. No normalization needed. The ORIGIN field in the invoice is a dedicated origin field (labeled 'ORIGIN') adjacent to the commodity description, satisfying the LC-exception requirement per rule 2.2 that origin must be in an explicit origin field.

evidence.section: ORIGIN field (dedicated field adjacent to DESCRIPTION)

rule_evaluation_log.details: Certificate contains 'LC NO: LC0999323000011' as a separate field label in box 6. Invoice contains 'L/C NO LC0999323000011' as a separate field label. Both use recognized bank-term designations ('LC NO', 'L/C NO') as separate labels with matching LC number. LC exception activated. Only country of origin will be verified.

Document A is Certificate of Origin (ICC/EU format). Document B is Commercial Invoice. Certificate file contains only the certificate. Invoice file contains only the invoice. Standard pairing applies.
Consignor CAMPANELLO AND CO AB matches invoice SELLER. Consignee GANZHOU RONGTONG INDUSTRIAL CO., LTD matches invoice BUYER. Contract number CSG 221216 matches. At least one criterion met.
Certificate origin: NORWAY. Invoice origin field: NORWAY. Exact match.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: LC reference might not be a genuine Letter of Credit but merely a reference number containing 'LC'
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: The certificate uses 'LC NO:' as a separate field label (not embedded in a reference number). The invoice uses 'L/C NO' as a separate field label. Both are recognized bank-term designations per rule 2.2. The LC number LC0999323000011 is a standalone value following the label, not part of a concatenated reference. The letters 'LC' are preceded by a field label designation and followed by a space and then the number, confirming genuine LC context.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Country of origin NORWAY might conflict with consignor country SWEDEN
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per rule 4.5, country of origin refers to where goods are manufactured/produced, not where the consignor is established. A Swedish consignor shipping Norwegian-origin goods is fully valid and normal in international trade. No ground for MISMATCH or MANUAL_REVIEW.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet innehåller en Letter of Credit-referens (LC0999323000011), varför verifieringen begränsas till ursprungsland. Certifikatet anger ursprungsland Norge och fakturan bekräftar samma ursprungsland i sitt dedikerade ursprungsfält. Dokumenten kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: LC exception activated based on LC NO: LC0999323000011 in certificate matching L/C NO LC0999323000011 in invoice. Verification limited to country of origin per rule 2.2. Country of origin NORWAY in certificate exactly matches NORWAY in invoice's dedicated ORIGIN field. All applicable checkpoints verified as MATCH. Result: IDENTICAL.

output_quality_checks.notes: All applicable checkpoints (country_of_origin) have status MATCH with confidence 0.99 (>0.85). comparison_result IDENTICAL is consistent. workflow_recommendation AUTO_APPROVAL_ELIGIBLE is consistent with IDENTICAL result.
